title: Permanent Residency
text:
Permanent Residency - SupportGoWhere
Permanent Residency
Share link
Allows eligible foreigners to apply for permanent residency.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Fees payable
Referral not required
Collapse all sections
About this support

Facilitates the process for eligible foreigners to reside in Singapore on a permanent basis

Permanent Residents aged 15 and above will be issued the Singapore blue Identity Card (IC)

Who is this for?

Available for:

Spouse of a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Unmarried child (aged below 21) born from a legal marriage to / have been legally adopted by a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident

Aged parent of a Singapore Citizen

Employment Pass or S Pass holder

Student studying in Singapore

Foreign investor in Singapore

Please note that the above criteria are not exhaustive. Individual eligibility is still subject to ICA’s approval.

What to expect?

Processing time for each application is at least 6 months

You may check your application status online through MyICA

A $100 non-refundable processing fee is payable for each applicant at the point of submission of application

If application is successful, you will need to complete permanent residency formalities personally at the ICA office

How to apply?

Refer to ICA's explanatory notes and prepare the required supporting documents

All permanent residence applications must be submitted online using the e-Service using your valid Singpass account, or your Foreign Identification Number and immigration pass issuance date if you're a student

For more information on e-Service accessibility, please refer to the Frequently Asked Questions and Step-by-Step guide in the e-Service

For further assistance, please contact ICA at 6391 6100 or submit an enquiry

Information last updated on 27 Feb 2026
